title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 13th July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Momentum intact at a 52-week high; hold with a trailing stop loss.
recommendations.detail: A stock that was at 110-120 until recently went up to 300 in just a month and is now consolidating at 280-285 even after the recent correction, at a 52-week high. He asked the caller whether the buy was short, medium or long term. If playing short-term momentum, compulsorily keep a trailing stop loss; if playing for the medium term, keep the stop somewhat more distant and hold the positions for now.

recommendations.note: Jumped and is back in consolidation at a 52-week high; hold with a trailing stop.
recommendations.detail: Like Standard Engineering, Capstone jumped and from that jump is going back into consolidation mode, and is also at a 52-week high. There is short-term momentum in both stocks. For a momentum play keep a compulsory trailing stop loss; for a medium-term view keep a wider stop and hold as of now.

recommendations.note: Definitely a buy; one of the better micro-electronics and defence-electronics names.
recommendations.detail: Astra is definitely one of the better companies in micro-electronics and defence electronics, trading at its 52-week high, and the last quarter result was extremely good. The demerger announcement and the board meeting are already done, so whether buying post-demerger unlocks further value and returns is difficult to say immediately. Even so he said definitely a buy, and there is definitely no need to sell Astra Micro.

recommendations.note: Decent MNC but long-term-only returns; no extraordinary wealth creation.
recommendations.detail: AstraZeneca Pharma is a multinational company without doubt and a decent company he would not say no to, but it only gives returns in the long term - one should not expect great returns in the short term. It is a high-priced stock trading above almost 8,000 (a Rs 2 paid-up share). A steady-performance company, but not one where extraordinary wealth creation will happen.

recommendations.note: Recovering after the Haryana fraud; Warburg Pincus gets majority equity by next September.
recommendations.detail: He had strongly called IDFC out as an investment idea long ago near 30-40 and the stock doubled. A fraud in Haryana - a nexus with the state government and corrupt bank employees - then knocked it from near 200 to below 60. It is slowly recovering and is back to 80-plus with the 52-week high nearby at 87. Warburg Pincus and other major investors get majority equity by next September, which will make it more like a multinational bank than a domestic bank. Hold.

recommendations.price: 27; book profits in the mid-30s to 40
recommendations.note: The worst is over; textiles improving - hold with patience and book profits in the mid-30s to 40.
recommendations.detail: Ex-rights the stock corrected below 20 and has since been slowly recovering, now stabilising at 27. In his view the worst is over in the stock, so hold it. Textile companies are also slowly improving, so with a little patience the stock should get back into the mid-30s to 40 [as heard], at which point profits can be booked.

recommendations.note: Recent activity may not sustain; hold with a trading stop around 80.
recommendations.detail: Activity increased recently but whether it sustains continuously is difficult to say - from around 60-65 on June 1 the stock went almost to 85-95 all at once and is now consolidating at 90. He would keep a trading stop loss and hold. The trading stop is slightly far away at 78, but even at those levels, around 80, keep a trading stop and hold.

recommendations.note: Underperformer, but hold as a steady large-dividend player with a good yield.
recommendations.detail: NHPC is definitely something of an underperformer: it went above 100 last year, corrected below 70, and is now stabilising at 80. A weak monsoon always affects stocks like NHPC because, despite diversified green power, hydro is ultimately the maximum revenue generator, so mild weakness is possible. But there is a regular large dividend play with a good yield, so instead of writing it off as an underperformer, hold it as a steady dividend player. It should not be sold at this rate.

recommendations.price: target 330-340 if the metal cycle turns
recommendations.note: Post-demerger the stock is at true value but has gone extremely low; hold.
recommendations.detail: After the demerger the stock has come to its true value, but it is at low levels and corrected recently to extremely low levels. If the metal cycle turns again, the stock will easily reach 330-340 levels too. Hold. (The caller's other stock, 'First Fintech', was not audible and went unanswered.)

recommendations.note: A reflection of the Indian capital market; definitely hold for the extreme long term.
recommendations.detail: LIC is stabilising at 440 after the ex-bonus. Since listing it gave a good selling opportunity only a couple of times, including above 1,000. More or less LIC is a reflection of the Indian capital market, because LIC itself is the biggest investor in it - a government entity and the largest domestic institutional player. For the extreme long term he would definitely say hold; at current levels, definitely hold without any doubt.

recommendations.price: long term near 1,275-1,300; momentum only above 1,450-1,460
recommendations.note: Buy after a 5-10% correction; it plays a band between ~1,250 and ~1,470.
recommendations.detail: Olectra is a buy, though not immediately - buy after a 5-10% correction from here. The stock plays continuously in a band: it comes close to 1,250-1,240, then goes again to 1,460-1,470 and faces resistance there. If playing for momentum, buy only after 1,450-1,460 is crossed this time. If buying for the long term, buy only when it comes closer to 1,275-1,280, that is, once it comes near 1,300.

recommendations.note: Momentum in a generics player; hold, but keep a trading stop into results.
recommendations.detail: Akums Drugs recovered recently, reaching 700 from 450-460. They are basically a generic player rather than a specialty-drugs company, and in generics they have notched up on good turnovers. As of now hold, since there is momentum. But if bought for the short term, definitely keep a trading stop loss, because it is result season and any disappointment in the result can correct the stock immediately.

recommendations.note: Buy for the long term; wait for the result, then start accumulating.
recommendations.detail: He would buy PPL Pharma for the long term. In the short term there may not be great returns and the stock is still in consolidation mode. Wait for the result too - the last result was very disappointing, but this time result expectations are good. If a somewhat better result comes, start accumulating then.

recommendations.note: Best of the times are over; large retail gives steady gains but no spectacular wealth.
recommendations.detail: Asked about buying for the long term (the caller noted falling promoter stake and rising FII/DII stakes), he said the best of the times are over in it. The stock corrected close to 100, then rose after the regular equity divestment to almost 130-140, and has come back into consolidation at 117-118. In the extreme long term, unless large retail companies also diversify well on the quick-commerce side and sustain it, they can give steady gains but there is no scene of them creating spectacular wealth - be it DMart or Vishal Mega Mart.

recommendations.note: Good promoter, corrected a lot and attractive now; accumulate slowly but check the result.
recommendations.detail: KP Energy is a buy because it is a good promoter. The stock has corrected a lot in recent times and is attractive right now. On the indicators the bottom is done at 250 and it is slowly inching up. Start accumulating slowly, but wait for and look at the result once. 'Buy - and definitely KP Energy.'

recommendations.note: A huge wealth-creating stock; definitely buy for the long term only.
recommendations.detail: Asked whether one can buy at the current market price, he called Vinati Organics a huge wealth-creating stock. But it is for the long term - definitely not for the short term. For the long term, definitely buy.

recommendations.note: Book partial profits; keep a trailing stop on the rest while momentum lasts.
recommendations.detail: A recently listed company (from the IPO) that languished for many days before a spike: from about 120 at March-ending, the stock doubled to 200-plus in just the April-May-June quarter. He said booking partial profits here is also advisable. For the rest, keep a trailing stop loss and hold, since there is momentum in the stock as of now. (The stock name was garbled in the captions and is flagged as heard.)
